title: Gunpowder Armory
description: "Flintlock" gun mod that adds a variety of guns.
body:
**REQUIRES GECKOLIB!**

### A VARIETY OF OLD GUNS LIKE NEVER BEFORE!

Gunpowder Armory adds 13 unique old guns, such as flintlock guns, but also matchlock, wheellock and even a few repeating guns! All of these use a unique loading process that uses your offhand, and each one is different from another in some way, shape or form. That's pretty cool!



You can join the Discord server [here](https://discord.gg/7zTwNq7kJT)!

You can view the Curseforge version [here](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/gunpowder-armory)!

---
## FAQ

_Q: How do I load the guns?_

**A: This information is in the ingame guidebooks. Most use the same loading process, which is using gunpowder, bullets, ramrod and another gunpowder, but some are different and use magazine systems instead (which is covered in vol. 2 arsenal). Mind that this process does require your offhand, you can quickly swap items with F by default.**

_Q: Is this compatible with X?_


**A: Probably, since the mod only uses geckolib as a required dependency, but it should be able to use ingots and materials from other mods, like steel, just fine as long as it has the "forge:ingots/steel" tag. Likewise, this mods steel should be able to be used in place of other mods steels, as long as they accept the same tag. Same with lead.**

_Q: What are the differences between matchlocks, flintlocks and wheellocks?_


**A: Convenience to use, with matchlocks being the worst and most prone to failure, and wheellocks being the best and almost never failing.**

_Q: Wouldn't repeating guns be OP in a mod like this?_

**A: It depends on the context, but in terms of accuracy and damage, muskets and most other guns actually do much better. Repeating guns are better for shooting faster and dealing more damage over time, however.**

_Q: Who is Sterling G?_

**A: He's a late-game trader NPC, he's not particularly relevant early in the mod.**

---




**_This mod may be used in videos and modpacks._**

Made with MCreator.
